name: Arkivalier Online (ao.dk)
coverage: Kirkebøger (church records), folketællinger (census), Sønderjyske personregistre (Standesamt civil registry)
access_method: Web browser, manual search by parish and year. Browse scanned pages of handwritten records.
relevance: Primary source for birth/death/marriage in Roagger (Roager) parish. Also covers Standesamt records for the German civil registry period (1874–1920).
limitations: Handwritten records require manual reading; no text search. Navigation requires knowledge of parish names and approximate dates.

name: FamilySearch.org
coverage: Indexed Danish parish records, census, emigration records, civil registration
region: Global, strong Danish coverage including Sønderjylland
access_method: Search by name, place, and date range. Many Danish records are indexed and text-searchable. Some records available only as browse-only images.
relevance: Faster than raw kirkebøger for indexed records. Good for confirming names, dates, and family relationships in the Roagger area.
limitations: Not all Danish records are indexed. Sønderjylland records may be catalogued under German parish names (Roager vs. Roagger). Free account required.

name: Verlustlisten des Deutschen Heeres im Weltkrieg 1914–1918
coverage: All German Army casualties 1914–1918: killed, wounded, missing, captured
access_method: Search by surname, optionally filtered by regiment or unit. Results show list number, page, and casualty type (gefallen, verwundet, vermisst, gefangen).
relevance: Definitive source for confirming casualties among disappeared military persons. Can confirm deaths, wounds, or capture for Poulsen, Petersen, Schwartz, Skopnik, and others.
limitations: Common surnames (Petersen, Hansen) return hundreds of results — regiment identification is critical for narrowing matches. Some lists are damaged or incomplete.

name: Historisk Sønderjylland / Encyclopædi / Sønderjylland i Krigen
coverage: Danish minority in German military service during WW1, local history of Sønderjylland parishes
access_method: Browse by name, parish, or topic. Some databases are partially indexed. Also see Historisk Samfund for Sønderjylland (https://www.telesko.dk/) and Museum Sønderjylland resources.
relevance: Curated for exactly this population — Danish-speaking men serving in the German army. May have records of men from Roagger parish. Includes memorial lists and community documentation.
limitations: Coverage varies; not all parishes or persons are included. Some resources are in print only (Sønderjyske Årbøger, memorial volumes).

name: Rigsarkivet / Sønderjyske Personregistre
type: Danish state archives — Sønderjylland civil registry
coverage: Standesamt records (German civil registration) for Sønderjylland: births, marriages, deaths
access_method: Browse by Standesamt (civil registry district) and year. Records are in German. Accessible through ao.dk interface.
relevance: Complements church records with civil birth/marriage/death registrations. Covers the exact period of Peter's letters. Records are in German, which can provide additional details not in kirkebøger.
limitations: Records are in German handwriting (Kurrent script). Requires knowledge of Standesamt district names. Partially overlaps with ao.dk kirkebøger.

name: Ancestry.dk / MyHeritage
coverage: Aggregated historical records, user-contributed family trees, indexed census and church records
access: subscription (some free records; MyHeritage has a free tier)
access_method: Search by name, place, and date range. User-contributed family trees may already identify Mærsk/Gad families from Roagger.
relevance: Cross-referenced family trees may already connect persons in the registry. Useful for secondary confirmation of findings from primary sources.
limitations: Subscription required for most records. User-contributed trees vary in quality and may contain errors. Should not be used as a primary source without verification.

name: Bundesarchiv (German Federal Archives)
coverage: Military service records, unit histories, regimental muster rolls (Stammrollen), deployment records
access: partially free (online finding aids); in-person or written request for specific records
access_method: Online search of finding aids at invenio.bundesarchiv.de. Specific records require written request (Benutzungsantrag) with unit and time period. Some WW1 unit histories digitized.
relevance: Regimental muster rolls are the definitive source for who served in which unit. If Peter's regiment is identified, the Stammrollen can confirm all military associates.
limitations: Many WW1 Prussian military records were destroyed in the 1945 bombing of Heeresarchiv Potsdam. Bavarian and some other records survived better. Written requests may take weeks. Physical visit to Freiburg may be necessary.
